Gold Star Plumbing & Drain — LSA Performance Report

Google Local Services Ads · CallRail Data
📅 Period: January 1 – April 15, 2026 📊 Source: Google Local Services Ads (571 calls) 🔄 Generated: April 17, 2026
Overview January February March April

Overview — January through April 2026

Monthly Volume by Outcome

Booking Rate Trend

Why Did Call Volume Change?

January → February ▲59%

Volume grew from 74 to 118 calls (+59%). February typically picks up as residents address maintenance deferred over the holidays. The booking rate improved from 41% to 49%, and out-of-scope calls rose (1 → 6), reflecting broader ad reach. Same-day availability flags remained low (2 → 3 calls), suggesting technician capacity was manageable.

February → March ▲129%

The largest jump of the period — 118 to 270 calls. March is peak season for plumbing (spring thaw, pre-summer water heater checks, remodels). Booking rate improved to 57%, the strongest month. However, out-of-scope calls peaked at 13 and availability flags hit 7, pointing to technician capacity strain. A sign to plan staffing ahead of the next spring surge.

March → April (partial, Apr 1–15)

At 109 calls in the first 15 days, April is pacing toward roughly 218 calls for the full month — below March's peak but still strong. Booking rate is 50% and April has zero missed calls, the best answer rate of the period. Out-of-scope calls dropped to 3, suggesting improved targeting.

Recommendations

Not-Booked Rate

All months exceed the acceptable threshold (57%, 45%, 38%, 47%). AM should review not-booked recordings monthly, coach CSRs on handling pricing objections and "I'll call back" responses. Target: push book rate above 60%.

Same-Day Availability

17 calls flagged across all months where tech was not same-day available. In March especially (7 flags), demand exceeded capacity. Proactively coordinate with the client on staffing during high-volume periods.

Out-of-Scope Calls

23 OOS calls total — wrong numbers, out-of-area, job inquiries. Review LSA service category settings and geographic radius to reduce wasted ad spend on non-converting callers.

CSR Competitor Referrals

3 instances where a CSR directed callers to another company. Even for OOS calls, CSRs should politely decline without redirecting. Address immediately in CSR training.

Lead Archiving

Client must archive booked leads in the Google LSA dashboard weekly. Unarchived booked leads may be re-billed and impact lead quality scores. AM to verify every Monday.

Budget Monitoring

Monitor internal LSA budget manually. When remaining balance drops to $300–$500, alert the AM immediately. Set a weekly calendar check when spend exceeds 80% of monthly allocation.

Flags & Alerts

🚨 Not-Booked Rate: 56.8% (42 of 74 calls) — Flagged to AM

42 answered calls did not convert to bookings. Exceeds the 10–20% threshold. AM to review the Not Booked tab below and follow up on recoverable calls.

✅ Unanswered Calls (30s+): 0 — No Flag

All calls were answered or abandoned before 30 seconds. No coverage gaps detected.

🕐 Tech Not Same-Day Available: 2 calls — Flagged to Specialist

Callers were offered future appointments instead of same-day service. Dates offered included: tomorrow. Specialist to task to AM in real time for follow-up and scheduling coordination.

⚑ CSR Referred Another Company: 2 instance(s) — Flagged to AM

CSR(s) Rachelle, Unknown directed caller(s) to a competitor or outside service. To be addressed in CSR coaching. See Flags tab for call details.

📋 Out-of-Scope Calls: 1

Breakdown: 1 Out of Service Area. Review LSA targeting (service categories, geographic radius) to reduce wasted spend on non-ideal callers.

Flags & Alerts

🚨 Not-Booked Rate: 44.9% (53 of 118 calls) — Flagged to AM

53 answered calls did not convert to bookings. Exceeds the 10–20% threshold. AM to review the Not Booked tab below and follow up on recoverable calls.

✅ Unanswered Calls (30s+): 0 — No Flag

All calls were answered or abandoned before 30 seconds. No coverage gaps detected.

🕐 Tech Not Same-Day Available: 3 calls — Flagged to Specialist

Callers were offered future appointments instead of same-day service. Dates offered included: tomorrow, Monday, Tomorrow. Specialist to task to AM in real time for follow-up and scheduling coordination.

📋 Out-of-Scope Calls: 6

Breakdown: 5 Wrong Number · 1 Out of Service Area. Review LSA targeting (service categories, geographic radius) to reduce wasted spend on non-ideal callers.

Flags & Alerts

🚨 Not-Booked Rate: 38.1% (103 of 270 calls) — Flagged to AM

103 answered calls did not convert to bookings. Exceeds the 10–20% threshold. AM to review the Not Booked tab below and follow up on recoverable calls.

✅ Unanswered Calls (30s+): 0 — No Flag

All calls were answered or abandoned before 30 seconds. No coverage gaps detected.

🕐 Tech Not Same-Day Available: 7 calls — Flagged to Specialist

Callers were offered future appointments instead of same-day service. Dates offered included: tomorrow, Monday, Wednesday. Specialist to task to AM in real time for follow-up and scheduling coordination.

📋 Out-of-Scope Calls: 13

Breakdown: 8 Wrong Number · 3 Out of Service Area · 2 Out of Scope. Review LSA targeting (service categories, geographic radius) to reduce wasted spend on non-ideal callers.

Flags & Alerts

🚨 Not-Booked Rate: 46.8% (51 of 109 calls) — Flagged to AM

51 answered calls did not convert to bookings. Exceeds the 10–20% threshold. AM to review the Not Booked tab below and follow up on recoverable calls.

✅ Unanswered Calls (30s+): 0 — No Flag

All calls were answered or abandoned before 30 seconds. No coverage gaps detected.

🕐 Tech Not Same-Day Available: 5 calls — Flagged to Specialist

Callers were offered future appointments instead of same-day service. Dates offered included: tomorrow, Tuesday, Monday, Saturday. Specialist to task to AM in real time for follow-up and scheduling coordination.

⚑ CSR Referred Another Company: 1 instance(s) — Flagged to AM

CSR(s) Stephen directed caller(s) to a competitor or outside service. To be addressed in CSR coaching. See Flags tab for call details.

📋 Out-of-Scope Calls: 3

Breakdown: 3 Wrong Number. Review LSA targeting (service categories, geographic radius) to reduce wasted spend on non-ideal callers.
